page: Amazon Web Services m3.medium is a general-purpose, third-generation virtual machine instance running on the Xen hypervisor. It features a single dedicated physical core and vCPU powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 4.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 4 GB local NVMe SSD.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower tiers of modern cloud compute resources. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1259.98, and the Geekbench single-core score is 668.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 91.97 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 11.78 GB/sec for large uncached read operations and 10.36 GB/sec for large uncached writes. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are limited, though the processor supports basic AVX and AES instructions.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the m3.medium offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio suitable for legacy workloads or lightweight utility tasks. However, its low baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps and poor multi-core scaling limit its utility for modern, high-throughput applications. It is best suited for basic web serving, lightweight background processes, or small-scale development environments that do not require high-performance computing or significant memory capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services m3.medium is a legacy general-purpose instance featuring a single dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 core running at 2.5 GHz on the Xen hypervisor. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM and a 4 GB local NVMe SSD. Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 668.0 and a stress-ng CPU score of 1259.98. Memory bandwidth is limited, reaching 11.78 GB/sec for large uncached reads and 10.36 GB/sec for large uncached writes. Lacking advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and possessing a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps, this instance is best suited for lightweight, non-critical workloads such as basic static web serving, simple utility scripts, or small development environments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m3.medium is a general-purpose instance featuring 1 vCPU, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a 4 GB local NVMe SSD. It runs on the Xen hypervisor and is suited for basic web serving and lightweight utility tasks.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m3.medium is a general-purpose instance featuring 1 vCPU, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a 4 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for lightweight utility workloads.
tagline: An entry-level general-purpose instance with a dedicated single-core processor and local NVMe storage for lightweight utility workloads.

4.0 GB of system memory and 4 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Xen hypervisor virtualization on x86_64 architecture
Best suited for basic static web serving and lightweight utility tasks
